what_it_does: Copies conversation JSONL files from per-project source trees into an archive when newer, indexes the copied conversations into a local embedding database, and queues or generates per-conversation summaries with sidecar sentinel files and a daily limit.
observations: Uses mtime-gated atomic copy via a temp file plus rename.
Maintains EXCLUSION_MARKERS and a shouldSkipConversation helper to skip indexing or summarizing flagged chats.
Extracts a session UUID from the filename basename for summary naming.
Indexes only newly copied files unless skipIndex is set.
Summary generation is capped by summaryLimit, defaulting to 10, and uses shouldQueueForSummary to decide what to process.
Writes an empty sentinel for zero-exchange conversations and a structured error sentinel when summarization fails.
syncAllSources iterates enabled CAPTURE_SOURCES rows and aggregates per-source SyncResult values.
standouts: Atomic copy with temp file and rename to avoid partial writes.
Registry-driven multi-source sync entry point that reuses the single-source pipeline.
Distinct empty and error summary sentinels to manage retry state.
conventions: Atomic file writes with process.pid temp names.
Sidecar sentinel files for summary state.
Dynamic async imports for heavy optional modules.
Per-project directory layout under source and archive roots.
Best-effort error handling that records failures but continues processing.
concerns: shouldSkipConversation reads the entire file into memory, and the same file may be read twice (once during copy/summary queue and again during indexing).
copyIfNewer leaves temp files on crash and assumes the temp and destination are on the same filesystem.
syncConversations combines copying, indexing, and summarization in one large function.
Summary failure sentinel writes swallow their own errors silently while still surfacing the original failure.
No concurrency control; all work is sequential.
notes: This is the archive/index/summary orchestrator: it mirrors conversation logs, embeds new ones, and produces capped, retry-aware summaries driven by a source registry.
questions: What exact rules do shouldQueueForSummary and formatErrorSentinel implement?
How are CAPTURE_SOURCES rows enabled or disabled, and what directories do they map to?
What embedding model and database backend do initEmbeddings and initDatabase use?
What is the contract of parseConversation and summarizeConversation?
How are excluded projects configured and updated?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a registry-driven multi-source sync that reuses the existing sync lane; the code does copy, index, and summarize across enabled capture sources and aggregates results, matching the comment.
apparent_themes: best-effort bounded summarization with durable state sentinels
intention_signals: The file is trying to build a reliable, incremental ingestion pipeline that treats conversation capture sources as a pluggable registry, persists summary state in sidecar sentinel files, and keeps expensive summarization bounded and retryable.
novelty: The registry-driven multi-source entry point that flattens enabled capture-source rows and aggregates results, plus the use of distinct empty and error summary sentinels to manage summary lifecycle.
contribution: Provides the central sync orchestration: mtime-gated atomic copy, per-file indexing into an embedding store, capped summary generation with durable sentinel state, and a registry-based multi-source driver that aggregates results across capture sources.
summary_for_embedding: sync.ts is the conversation archive orchestrator that copies newer JSONL logs per project, indexes them into an embedding database, and generates limited, retry-aware summaries using sidecar sentinel files, with a registry-driven multi-source entry point.
intention_for_embedding: To incrementally ingest, persist, and summarize captured AI conversations from multiple sources while avoiding reprocessing and bounding expensive summarization work.

symbols.does: Reads a JSONL file and returns true if it contains any exclusion marker.
symbols.description: A small guard that inspects file content for markers such as DO NOT INDEX THIS CHAT, NO_INSIGHTS_FOUND, or the summarizer context marker, so flagged conversations can be skipped during indexing and summarization.

symbols.does: Type describing counts and errors returned by sync operations.
symbols.description: Defines the aggregated outcome shape: copied, skipped, indexed, summarized counts plus an array of per-file error records.

symbols.does: Type for optional sync behavior flags and limits.
symbols.description: Carries skipIndex, skipSummaries, and summaryLimit options that callers use to disable stages or cap the number of summaries generated per run.

symbols.does: Copies a source file to a destination only when newer, using an atomic temp-and-rename.
symbols.description: Ensures the destination directory exists, compares mtimes, and performs an atomic copy via a pid-suffixed temp file renamed into place, returning whether a copy actually happened.

symbols.does: Pulls the last UUID out of a JSONL filename.
symbols.description: Uses a global UUID regex on the filename stem to extract a session identifier, used to tag or name summaries for a conversation.

symbols.does: Main per-source pipeline: mirror, index, and summarize conversation files.
symbols.description: Walks project subdirectories of a source tree, copies newer JSONL files to the archive, indexes newly copied files into the embedding database, and queues then generates summaries up to the configured limit while writing empty or error sentinels as needed.

symbols.does: Registry-driven entry point that runs syncConversations across enabled capture sources and aggregates results.
symbols.description: Flattens enabled capture-source directories from the registry and invokes the single-source sync for each, summing copied, skipped, indexed, summarized counts and collecting all errors into one SyncResult.
